item.title: Ebola kills 3 Red Cross workers in the Congo, organization says
item.description: The three workers, who fell ill in March and died in May, were "among the first known victims of the outbreak," the Red Cross said.

item.title: Trump says U.S. is "getting a lot closer" to agreement with Iran
item.description: U.S. officials and negotiators were discussing a process to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, sources briefed on the conversations said.

item.title: Trump says U.S. will only sign Iran agreement "if we get everything we want"
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio said "there might be some news a little later today" when asked about the Iran negotiations.

item.title: Serbian protesters clash with police amid anti-government rally in Belgrade
item.description: Protesters on Saturday streamed into central Belgrade, many carrying banners and wearing T-shirts inscribed with the "Students win" motto of the youth movement.

item.title: What to know about California chemical tank set to explode or leak
item.description: The tank at GKN Aerospace is estimated to contain 7,000 gallons of methyl methacrylate, a volatile chemical used to produce plastics.

item.title: Trump administration to require most green card applicants to leave U.S. first
item.description: The Trump administration on Friday announced a sweeping policy designed to make it harder for immigrants already in the U.S. to get permanent residency.

item.title: Staten Island shipyard explosion, fire leaves 1 dead, over 30 hurt
item.description: One person died and over 30 people, mostly FDNY members, were hurt in an explosion and fire at a Staten Island shipyard Friday.

item.title: Trump administration announces major changes for immigrants seeking green cards
item.description: The Trump administration announced Friday its sweeping policy change that will require most immigrants seeking green cards to leave the U.S. first. CBS News' Camilo Montoya-Galvez has more.

item.title: Over 40,000 evacuated in California chemical leak as officials warn tank may explode
item.description: Southern California officials have expanded evacuations in several Orange County cities due to concerns over a leaking toxic chemical tank that they say is "actively in crisis."

item.title: Coal mine explosion in China kills at least 82 people, state media say
item.description: The incident marks China's deadliest mining accident in years.

item.title: Kyle Busch's cause of death revealed by family
item.description: Kyle Busch, who won more races in NASCAR's top three series than anyone in history, died suddenly on Thursday.

item.title: Moo's Craft Barbecue brings Texas flavor to Los Angeles
item.description: "CBS Saturday Morning" explores by Moo's Craft Barbecue has become Los Angeles' most popular barbecue spot.

item.title: NASCAR star Kyle Busch dies at 41 after a severe illness, his family says
item.description: Kyle Busch's family earlier Thursday announced he had been hospitalized with a "severe illness."

item.title: NASCAR star Kyle Busch hospitalized with severe illness, family says
item.description: Two-time NASCAR Cup Series champion Kyle Busch has been hospitalized with a severe illness and won't compete at Charlotte Motor Speedway this weekend, his family said.

item.title: Summer movies worth rushing to the theater for
item.description: Matt Singer, the editor and critic for ScreenCrush.com, joins "CBS Saturday Morning" for a preview of this summer's hottest movies worth watching.

item.title: Ryan Bingham reflects on his award-winning career
item.description: Ryan Bingham's career spans decades and genres. He went from a 2009 awards season sweep with "The Weary Kind (Theme from Crazy Heart)" to starring as Walker in "Yellowstone." Bingham sat down with "CBS Saturday Morning" to reflect on his work and to discuss his latest project, "They Call Us The Lucky Ones," which he created in collaboration with The Texas Gentlemen.

item.title: Lamorne Morris steps into the Marvel universe
item.description: Lamorne Morris sits down with "CBS Saturday Morning" to discuss his career as he steps into the Marvel universe with Nicholas Cage for "Spider-Noir."

item.title: Exclusive discounts from CBS Mornings Deals
item.description: On this edition of CBS Mornings Deals, we show you items that might just become essentials in your everyday life. Visit cbsdeals.com to take advantage of these exclusive deals today. CBS earns commissions on purchases made through cbsdeals.com.

item.title: Saturday Sessions: Ryan Bingham and The Texas Gentlemen perform "Relevance"
item.description: Ryan Bingham is an Oscar, Grammy, Golden Globe and Critics' Choice award-winning singer-songwriter who also played Walker in "Yellowstone." For his first album in seven years, Bingham teamed up with The Texas Gentlemen to create "They Call Us The Lucky Ones." Here's Ryan Bingham and The Texas Gentlemen performing "Relevance."

item.title: Saturday Sessions: Ryan Bingham and The Texas Gentlemen perform "Let the Big Dog Eat"
item.description: Ryan Bingham is an Oscar, Grammy, Golden Globe and Critics' Choice award-winning singer-songwriter who also played Walker in "Yellowstone." For his first album in seven years, Bingham teamed up with The Texas Gentlemen to create "They Call Us The Lucky Ones." Here's Ryan Bingham and The Texas Gentlemen performing "Let the Big Dog Eat."

item.title: Saturday Sessions: Ryan Bingham and The Texas Gentlemen perform "The Lucky Ones"
item.description: Ryan Bingham is an Oscar, Grammy, Golden Globe and Critics' Choice award-winning singer-songwriter who also played Walker in "Yellowstone." For his first album in seven years, Bingham teamed up with The Texas Gentlemen to create "They Call Us The Lucky Ones." Here's Ryan Bingham and The Texas Gentlemen performing "The Lucky Ones."

item.title: Money expert answers some of your top finance questions
item.description: CBS News' business analyst Jill Schlesinger answers some burning finance questions, from investments to home loans.

item.title: SpaceX launches revamped Super Heavy-Starship rocket ahead of IPO
item.description: SpaceX launched its revamped Super Heavy-Starship rocket on Friday, with some minor issues during the test, ahead of the company's highly anticipated initial public offering.

item.title: U.S. tightens Ebola travel restrictions as new cases emerge abroad
item.description: The U.S. has tightened its Ebola travel restrictions as more cases emerge in Africa and health officials battle misinformation about the virus.

item.title: Woman allegedly opens fire outside Raleigh courthouse, hospitalizes 2 lawyers
item.description: A woman is accused of opening fire outside a courthouse in Raleigh, North Carolina, and shooting two lawyers, police said.

item.title: Details emerge on sudden illness that led to Kyle Busch's shocking death
item.description: More details are emerging about the sudden illness that led to NASCAR legend Kyle Busch's shocking death at 41.

item.title: U.S. expects record-setting travel despite high gas prices
item.description: Nearly 40 million Americans are expected on the roads for Memorial Day weekend, AAA estimates, despite the highest gas prices in four years.

item.title: Eye Opener: Record-setting travel expected for Memorial Day weekend
item.description: A record number of Americans are expected to travel for Memorial Day weekend despite high gas prices. All that and all that matters in today's Eye Opener.

item.title: 5/23: Saturday Morning
item.description: Record-setting travel is expected in the U.S. over Memorial Day weekend despite high gas prices.

item.title: Rob Base, rapper on classic hip-hop track "It Takes Two," dies at 59
item.description: Rob Base, the MC whose lyrics lit up the dancefloor classic "It Takes Two" and got countless people moving worldwide, passed away Friday.
